description: Primary incident tracking list for the Enterprise Incident Tracker. Each row represents one incident across its full lifecycle, spanning all five enterprise domains.

columns.name: Title
columns.description: Incident title — brief, descriptive summary of the incident.

columns.name: ItemID
columns.description: Unique identifier in EIT-NNN format. Auto-generated during intake promotion via concat('EIT-', string(ID)).

columns.name: Owner
columns.description: Display name of the person responsible for managing this incident. Free text — not a People picker.

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: Enterprise domain that owns this incident. Primary taxonomy column. Replaces OFR FunctionalGroup. Must match EIT_DomainContacts.Title exactly for escalation flow lookups.

columns.name: SubDomain
columns.description: Optional sub-category within the domain (e.g., 'Insider Risk' within Physical Security, 'Data Privacy' within Privacy & Technology). Free text.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: ERM-aligned 5-tier severity classification. Set during triage and refined during incident lifecycle. Distinct from Priority (which reflects operational urgency). Used by Power Automate to calculate EscalationThreshold.

columns.name: Priority
columns.description: Operational triage priority set by the submitter. Drives day-to-day queue ordering in the Tracker screen. Distinct from Severity (which is the ERM-aligned classification).

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Current lifecycle status. 'Contained' is added vs OFR — used when the immediate threat is neutralised but the incident is still open for follow-up. Power Automate staleness flow excludes Contained and Closed items.

columns.name: IncidentType
columns.description: Enterprise incident taxonomy category. Standardises classification across all domains. Defined in EIT-Enterprise-Taxonomy.md.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: Originating system for this incident record. Phase 2 integration hook — when Power Automate connectors to ServiceNow or iSight are built, they set this field to identify origin and prevent duplicate tracking.

columns.name: DateRaised
columns.description: Date the incident was first identified. Set to utcNow() during intake promotion.

columns.name: DateContained
columns.description: Date the incident was contained or closed. Populated by Power Apps when Status is set to 'Contained' or 'Closed'. Enables Mean Time to Contain (MTTC) leadership KPI calculation.

columns.name: LastUpdated
columns.description: Timestamp of the most recent update. Reset to Now() each time an update is added via the Issue Detail screen.

columns.name: NextAction
columns.description: Description of the next steps. Populated from intake Description during promotion.

columns.name: EscalationThreshold
columns.description: Standardised escalation status. Calculated daily by Power Automate: Critical + 14 days stale = Executive Brief; High + 7 days stale = Escalate. Can also be set manually in the Issue Detail screen. Drives the EIT Domain Escalation Notifier flow.

columns.name: DaysSinceUpdate
columns.description: Integer. Days elapsed since LastUpdated. Calculated daily by Power Automate staleness flow. Reset to 0 when an update is added.

columns.name: StalenessFlag
columns.description: Set by Power Automate daily: 0-7 days = Current, 8-14 days = Aging, 15+ days = Stale. Used for colour-coded staleness badges in the Tracker and Kanban screens.

columns.name: ConfidentialityLevel
columns.description: Data sensitivity classification for this incident record. Internal = general firm use. Restricted = limited to domain leads and above. Confidential = need-to-know only. Phase 2 will use this to enforce row-level visibility filters.

columns.name: RelatedItemIDs
columns.description: Comma-separated list of related EIT-NNN identifiers. Enables cross-domain incident linking. Displayed as navigable references in the Issue Detail screen. Phase 2 integration hook for cross-incident graph analytics.

columns.name: PrivilegedAndConfidential
columns.description: Legal privilege designation. When Yes, this incident is subject to attorney-client privilege and/or work product protection. Distinct from ConfidentialityLevel — P&C is a legal status with discovery and disclosure implications, not merely a sensitivity tier. An incident can be simultaneously Confidential AND Privileged & Confidential. Setting this flag should trigger item-level SharePoint permission restrictions — see EIT-Access-Control-Guide.md. Can only be set or cleared by a domain lead, legal counsel, or EIT administrator.

relationships.toHistory: EIT_Incidents.ItemID is referenced by EIT_IncidentHistory.ParentItemID (logical link, not enforced by SharePoint)
relationships.toDomainContacts: EIT_Incidents.Domain.Value must match EIT_DomainContacts.Title for escalation flow lookups to function correctly

notes: Total: 20 columns (11 retained/adapted from OFR_Issues, 9 net-new)
Power Apps Patch formulas must use {Value: 'text'} syntax for all Choice columns
Severity and Priority are intentionally separate: Severity = ERM-aligned classification, Priority = operational triage
Items with Status 'Contained' or 'Closed' are excluded from the staleness and escalation calculations in Power Automate
